claim: Middle-positioned information suffers 10-40% reduced recall accuracy in long-context experiments
notes: Percentages vary by model family and context length. Revalidate when new model generations release.

claim: Multi-agent systems cost 10-50x more tokens than single-agent chat for complex tasks
source: Production deployment data and LangGraph benchmarks
notes: Multiplier depends heavily on coordination pattern and task complexity. Revalidate with new orchestration frameworks.

claim: Reducing specialized tools to a smaller primitive tool set improved measured outcomes in production
notes: Specific reduction ratios vary by domain.

claim: Token usage, tool calls, and model choice dominate performance variance in browsing-agent evaluations
notes: Findings may not generalize to non-browsing agent tasks.

claim: Even a single distractor document triggers measurable performance degradation
